etymology.description: Depicts various interpretations: 1) shoulders, 2) the tail of a fish, 3) part of a chariot, 4) pedestal, 5) small table
etymology.interpretation: The original pictorial meaning of 丙(bǐng) is uncertain but has been traditionally identified in multiple ways, including physical objects like shoulders, fish tails, or chariot parts. Its precise original depiction is obscure and no single interpretation is universally accepted. It became associated with the third of the ten Heavenly Stems, losing its original pictorial significance.

examples.chinese: 丙是一种元素(丙是一種元素)。
丙在化学中有特定的用途(丙在化學中有特定的用途)。
examples.pinyin: Bǐng zài huàxué zhōng yǒu tèdìng de yòngtú.
examples.english: Bing has specific uses in chemistry.

examples.chinese: 丙与其他元素结合时(丙與其他元素結合時)，会发生有趣的反应(會發生有趣的反應)。
examples.pinyin: Bǐng yǔ qítā yuánsù jiéhé shí, huì fāshēng yǒuqù de fǎnyìng.
examples.english: When bing combines with other elements, interesting reactions occur.
